name: implementation-start
description: Prepare one selected active-spec task for implementation by composing authoritative lifecycle context without mutation.

arguments.name: spec_id
arguments.description: Active spec package ID or path.

arguments.name: task_id
arguments.description: Stable selected task ID, for example T003.

arguments.name: repo_root
arguments.description: Optional repository root when it cannot be inferred from the session.

instructions: Use the spec-lifecycle-manager skill and the selected spec and task as the bounded implementation slice.
Use developer-start for first-run repository orientation; this prompt prepares an already selected active-spec task for implementation.

Call active_spec_preflight first, then compose task_context and traceability_lookup so requirements, acceptance criteria, design sections, verification expectations, durable targets, open decisions, and dependency state remain independently inspectable.
Use agent_readiness_packet and stage_readiness as existing readiness authorities. Do not create a second readiness decision or a shared-core aggregate.
Treat validation_plan output as planned validation, never completed evidence.
Preserve every blocker reported by a source. Do not report ready to implement while any blocker remains; return the smallest concrete next action that can resolve or route each blocker.
If no active spec exists, use no_active_spec_context and do not invent a spec or task.
If the selected task is not runnable, use next_task to report a valid alternative or concrete blockers; do not invent a runnable task.
Keep this prompt read-only. Do not mutate task state, edit lifecycle artifacts, or write evidence.
Return MCP tools as the primary agent actions. Put repo-relative CLI equivalents only in validation_or_recovery_commands and include a fallback_reason.
Preserve the source tool and interface provenance for composed facts. Return identifiers and bounded summaries, not semantic source content.
client_support_recovery: Use MCP active_spec_preflight, task_context, traceability_lookup, agent_readiness_packet, stage_readiness, validation_plan, no_active_spec_context, and next_task when available. If MCP tools are unavailable, identify mcp_unavailable as the fallback_reason and use the repo-relative skills/spec-lifecycle-manager/scripts/spec_runtime.py equivalents for validation or explicit recovery; do not treat fallback execution or planned validation as completed evidence.
